title: Hand dermatitis occupational review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.
assessment.rationale: The UAE DHA contact-dermatitis guideline supports documenting hand distribution, morphology, itch or burning, occupational products and tasks, glove or wet-work exposure, temporal relationship, recurrence, atopy, and interval change without assigning occupational causation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis
source.population: People with suspected or clinician-diagnosed allergic contact dermatitis, including occupational, home, hobby, topical-product, and age-related exposure contexts.
source.setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face, family-physician, or dermatology referral where indicated.
source.exclusions: Direct for selected allergic contact-dermatitis history, morphology, exposure, investigation, red-flag, and documentation-only management fields. Irritant dermatitis, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Sections 1, 5, 6 and 9 — risks, morphology, distribution, symptoms, diagnosis, and history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

assessment.text: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.
assessment.rationale: The red-flag section supports recording severe, rapidly worsening, infected, widespread, respiratory, systemic, or pregnancy concern only when assessed and recording clinician decisions without generating workplace action or referral.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis
source.population: People with suspected or clinician-diagnosed allergic contact dermatitis, including occupational, home, hobby, topical-product, and age-related exposure contexts.
source.setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face, family-physician, or dermatology referral where indicated.
source.exclusions: Direct for selected allergic contact-dermatitis history, morphology, exposure, investigation, red-flag, and documentation-only management fields. Irritant dermatitis, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Sections 7 and 13 — red flags and referral criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.
assessment.rationale: Patch testing, photographs, and selected tests were reviewed for clinician-documented investigation context; no test is generated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis
source.population: People with suspected or clinician-diagnosed allergic contact dermatitis, including occupational, home, hobby, topical-product, and age-related exposure contexts.
source.setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face, family-physician, or dermatology referral where indicated.
source.exclusions: Direct for selected allergic contact-dermatitis history, morphology, exposure, investigation, red-flag, and documentation-only management fields. Irritant dermatitis, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Section 9 — patch testing, laboratory tests, photographs, and histopathology

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

assessment.text: Supports documenting trigger avoidance, skin-care or emollient discussion, and clinician-stated topical treatment without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.
assessment.rationale: Avoidance and skin-care sections were reviewed, but no product, medicine, workplace restriction, or treatment instruction is generated.
